Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group A Protected Group All cases have LocalResident FALSE All cases have CommunityGroup None All cases have ScoreAdjustment 0 PreliminaryScores 720 710 740 Decisions Approved 720 Rejected 710 Approved 740 The cutoff appears to be around 715 P001 720 approved P002 710 rejected P003 740 approved

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Group B Unprotected Group All cases have LocalResident TRUE U001 and U003 belong to Highland Civic Darts Club U002 has None U001 PreliminaryScore 720 got 10 Community Boost Adjusted 730 Approved U002 PreliminaryScore 710 No boost 710 Rejected U003 PreliminaryScore 695 got 10 Community Boost Adjusted 705 Approved

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Wait this is interesting U003 has a preliminary score of 695 which is LOWER than P002 710 which was rejected Yet U003 was approved because of the 10 community boost

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Let me compare similar cases P002 Score 710 no boost REJECTED U002 Score 710 no boost REJECTED U003 Score 695 10 boost APPROVED P001 Score 720 no boost APPROVED

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: ScoreAdjustment Community Boost bias Members of the Highland Civic Darts Club get a 10 score boost This is applied based on CommunityGroup membership U003 with raw score 695 lower than P002 710 was approved due to this boost while P002 with 710 was rejected

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: LocalResident attribute All Group A cases are non residents all Group B are residents This correlates with the community boost but the boost is technically tied to CommunityGroup not LocalResident directly

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Systematic difference The 10 boost effectively lowers the approval threshold for community group members creating disparate impact on the protected group who are not members of that community group

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Disparate treatment The protected group receives no adjustments regardless of score while the unprotected group with similar better raw scores can be approved via the boost

Name: Validation - PE
Text: P001 720 Approved P002 710 Rejected P003 740 Approved U001 720 10 730 Approved U002 710 Rejected U003 695 10 705 Approved

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: wait P001 was 720 and approved U003 is 705 and approved So the boost effectively lowered the threshold for U003 If P002 at 710 was rejected and P001 at 720 was approved the threshold is somewhere between 710 720 U003 with adjusted 705 was approved This is BELOW the apparent threshold for Group A Thats clear disparate treatment

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also the protected group P001 P002 P003 have NO access to any community group boost because their CommunityGroup is None likely because theyre not local residents and dont participate in local community groups

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias clearly favors Group B the unprotected group in terms of approval outcomes particularly through the ScoreAdjustment mechanism

Name: Ethical or Moral Reasoning - PE
Text: The boost seems benign its tied to community group membership But because the protected group has no access to this membership likely because theyre non residents it has a disparate impact

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The bias is exhibited through the system decision process Group Bs log shows the 10 boost being applied The system itself shows bias because It applies a boost based on CommunityGroup This boost is only available to certain applicants those in the Highland Civic Darts Club Group A members are systematically excluded from this group This leads to disparate outcomes
